title: betula utilis subsp. utilis
intro: Betula utilis subsp. utilis, also known as the Himalayan white birch, is a subspecies of the Betula utilis tree, native to the Himalayas and the Tibetan Plateau. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Ayurvedic and Tibetan medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Betula utilis subsp. utilis include reducing fever, alleviating rheumatism and arthritis, and acting as a natural diuretic. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments.
